# Lettuce

> Lettuce is an agent-operated, local-first markdown+git protocol for giving an operator's agent durable company context that is separate from personal memory, owned by the operator, and portable across agent runtimes.

You are reading this because an operator gave you one link and asked you to set up Lettuce. Your job is to guide the human through setup, not to make them learn the CLI and not to silently create a repo before they understand what is happening.

## First Response To The Operator

Before installing or running setup, briefly say:

> Lettuce gives your agent a local markdown+git work brain for one company/project. It keeps that work context separate from personal memory, records which sources should feed it, and lets the agent keep the context fresh from signals it can access. The point is not to make you review every signal. The point is to give your agent durable company context it can use later.

Then ask these setup questions and wait for answers/confirmation:

1. Which work context is this for? (company, project, client, newsletter, etc.)
2. Should I start with a local repo first, or use an existing repo/path?
3. What signal sources should eventually feed it? (chat/direct notes, email, transcripts, GitHub/Linear/Notion/Slack, local files, etc.)
4. What sources can I access now, and what should be marked as later/needs setup?
5. What cadence or trigger should keep it fresh? (manual for now, daily check, after meetings, when asked, etc.)
6. What one small meaningful sample signal should I use to calibrate the first run?

If the operator already gave some of this context, summarize your assumptions and ask for confirmation. Do not silently infer the whole setup.

## Agent Setup Instructions

- Install Lettuce from the repo URL only after the operator confirms setup: `python3 -m pip install 'git+https://github.com/kenseals/lettuce.git'`.
- Create or choose a local Lettuce repo for one confirmed organization/work context, not for the operator's personal memory.
- Record source intent for the sources discussed with the operator, including whether each is `available_now`, `needs_setup`, `defer`, or manual-only.
- Ingest one small operator-approved sample signal first. Do not use the setup request itself as the sample unless the operator explicitly says to.
- Run handlers so the repo has a first useful brain/context update. Do not make the human review every signal by default.
- Preserve provenance, consent basis, source surface, and org/operator scope.
- Do not build duplicate chat, email, browser, OAuth, or service integrations inside Lettuce. Use your runtime's existing access, then write Lettuce stream events.
- End onboarding with a concise handoff: repo path, configured sources and their status, cadence/trigger model, what the first run added, how to use Lettuce next, and the next useful setup step.

Use the review commands only for optional calibration, sensitive sources, uncertain/high-impact updates, or if the operator explicitly asks to approve changes before they land. Review is a safety valve, not the core product loop.

## Start Here

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/README.md): Human-facing overview and shortest command path.
- [Agent-operated onboarding](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/agent-operated-onboarding.md): The primary setup workflow for agents.
- [First-run demo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/first-run-demo.md): Public-safe install-to-first-context walkthrough.
- [OpenClaw Lettuce skill](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/skills/openclaw-lettuce/SKILL.md): Use this if you are OpenClaw.

## Protocol Reference

- [Handler format](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/HANDLERS.md): How markdown handlers subscribe, publish, and return JSON.
- [OpenClaw runtime source setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/openclaw-runtime-source-setup.md): Boundary between OpenClaw-owned runtime access and Lettuce-owned protocol state.
- [Source discovery recipes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/source-discovery-recipes.md): How to record source intent and sample safely.
- [Company context for agents](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/company-context-for-agents.md): Product framing and why this exists.

## Optional

- [Quickstart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/QUICKSTART.md): Longer CLI walkthrough.
- [Try this first](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/TRY_THIS_FIRST.md): Short smoke-test path.
- [Public v0 release checklist](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/docs/public-v0-release-checklist.md): Current readiness and deferred work.
- [Full context bundle](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kenseals/lettuce/main/llms-full.txt): Single-file expanded agent setup context.
